originalTitle: From escaping child marriage 'to an old pervert' to becoming Sierra Leone's first lady
title: Meet Sierra Leone's Super Brave First Lady!
content:
Hello, Little Superstars!

Hi everyone! It's your friendly kid-reporter for Africa, ready with an amazing story just for you! We're going to travel with our minds to a beautiful country in West Africa called Sierra Leone. It's a place with sunny skies, pretty beaches, and lots of friendly children like you! Today, we have a super special story about a very important lady. She is a true hero, and her story will make your heart feel warm and strong. Are you ready to hear about her amazing journey?

Meet a Very Special Lady!

Our story is about Mrs. Fatima Maada Bio. She is the First Lady of Sierra Leone! Do you know what a First Lady is? She is the wife of the President, who is the leader of the country. So, Mrs. Fatima is like a very important helper to the President, Mr. Julius Maada Bio. Together, they work hard to make Sierra Leone a wonderful place for everyone. Mrs. Fatima is not just any lady; she is a powerful woman with a kind heart. She uses her special position to help lots and lots of people, especially children and women. She believes in making sure everyone has a chance to be happy and healthy. Let's find out more about her incredible life!

A Brave Start to Her Story

When Mrs. Fatima was just a little girl, like some of you, she faced a very big challenge. Someone much, much older wanted her to get married when she was still too young to understand what marriage really meant. This was a very big thing for such a young girl! She wanted to go to school and learn, play with friends, and dream about her future, not get married so early. But Fatima was very brave! She knew in her heart that she wanted to learn, read books, and go to school first. She chose to say 'no' to marrying when she was just a child. She chose her dreams and her books instead! It took a lot of courage for her to make that decision, but she believed in herself. **Did you know?** Going to school is super important for every child in the world! It helps you learn new things, make friends, and grow up to be smart and strong!

Dreams and Hard Work Pay Off!

Because of her bravery, Mrs. Fatima was able to go to school. She worked very, very hard. She learned so much, from reading amazing stories to understanding numbers. She grew up into a smart and wonderful woman, full of ideas and dreams. She traveled and learned even more, making herself very knowledgeable. Later, she met a kind and clever man named Mr. Julius Maada Bio. He became her husband, and guess what? He later became the President of Sierra Leone! And that's how Mrs. Fatima became the First Lady. Her journey shows us that if you are brave and work hard, your dreams can really come true! **Simple explanation:** A President is like the main captain of a big ship, but instead of a ship, it's a whole country! They help make rules and plans to keep everyone safe and happy.

What Does a First Lady Do?

Now, as the First Lady, Mrs. Fatima has a very important job. She doesn't just sit in a fancy house; she travels all around Sierra Leone, meeting people and listening to their stories. She helps make plans to solve problems and make life better for everyone. She is like a super helper for the whole country! She has special projects that she cares about a lot. She wants every child to feel safe and loved. She wants every girl to have the chance to go to school and not be forced to marry too early. She also works hard to make sure mothers and babies are healthy. She is truly a champion for her country! **Did you know?** Sierra Leone is known for its beautiful, sandy beaches where you can see amazing sunsets!

Helping Girls and Boys Learn!

One of the most important things Mrs. Fatima does is help children get an education. She wants *all* boys and girls to stay in school, just like she did. She knows that when children learn, they become smart adults who can help their country grow. She tells girls that their books are their best friends, and learning can take them anywhere! She has a big project called 'Hands Off Our Girls.' This project teaches everyone that girls should be protected and allowed to finish school before they think about marriage. It's all about letting girls be girls and boys be boys, enjoying their childhood and learning as much as they can.

A Champion for Health

Mrs. Fatima also cares a lot about everyone's health. She visits hospitals and clinics to see if people are getting good care. She helps make sure there are enough medicines and doctors, especially for moms and tiny babies. She wants every child to grow up strong and healthy, without getting sick. She teaches people about eating healthy food and keeping clean to stay safe from germs. She knows that healthy children can learn better and play more! Her work helps families all over Sierra Leone live happier, healthier lives. **Did you know?** Sierra Leone's capital city is called Freetown! It's a very busy and exciting place with lots of cars, people, and markets!

A Message for All of You!

Mrs. Fatima Maada Bio's story teaches us so many wonderful lessons. It tells us to be brave when things are hard. It teaches us to always choose our dreams and work hard for them. And it shows us that with kindness and courage, we can help make our communities and our countries better places for everyone. So, remember Mrs. Fatima's story! Be brave, learn lots in school, and always try to help others. You, too, can grow up to do amazing things, just like Sierra Leone's incredible First Lady! You are all little stars, and your future is bright!

Let's Celebrate!

What an inspiring story, right? Mrs. Fatima Maada Bio is a true example of courage, determination, and kindness. Let's celebrate her and all the wonderful work she does for the children and people of Sierra Leone. Until next time, keep shining bright!
summary: Mrs. Fatima Maada Bio, the First Lady of Sierra Leone, is a brave woman who chose education over marrying very young, and now she helps all children, especially girls, go to school and be healthy in her country.
whyMatters: Her amazing story teaches us to be brave, work hard for our dreams, and always help others in our community, showing that everyone can grow up to do great things and make the world better.

originalTitle: How worrying is the Ebola outbreak in DR Congo?
title: Helping Our Friends in DR Congo: Stopping a Tricky Germ!
content:
Hello, amazing kids! Welcome to your news report from Africa! Today, we have important news from a country called DR Congo. It's a beautiful place, just like many of our homes in Africa. Sometimes, people can get sick. You know how you might get a cold or a cough sometimes? Well, there's a different kind of sickness that some grown-ups are working very, very hard to stop in DR Congo. It's called Ebola. It's a big word, but we're going to learn about it together in a simple way.

What is Ebola?

Ebola is caused by a tiny, tiny germ. You can't see this germ with your eyes, not even if you squint really hard! It's so small, you need a special magnifying glass called a microscope to see it. Imagine something even smaller than a tiny ant – that's how small germs are! This germ can make people feel very sick. It's like having a really, really strong flu. People might feel very tired, have a fever (which means their body gets very hot), and their tummies can hurt a lot. It makes them feel very weak, and they need a lot of help to get better and strong again. **Did you know?** Germs are everywhere around us, on our hands, toys, and even in the air! But don't worry, most of them are harmless, and our bodies are very good at fighting off the bad ones!

Where is DR Congo?

DR Congo is a very big and beautiful country. It's right in the middle of our wonderful continent, Africa! If you look at a map of Africa, you'll see it right there in the heart, like a big, green patch. It's a place with big rivers, like the mighty Congo River, tall green trees that make up huge forests, and lots of different animals, including elephants and gorillas. Just like our own countries, it's full of amazing people, families, and communities. The people there are just like us, and they need our kind thoughts and support right now.

What is an "Outbreak"?

Imagine if suddenly many kids in your classroom all got the sniffles at the same time. That would be like a tiny 'outbreak' of sniffles! An Ebola outbreak is similar, but it's much more serious because it's a very strong germ. It means that many people in one area have started to get this particular sickness all at once. When this happens, it's like a big alarm goes off, telling all the doctors, nurses, and health helpers from everywhere to come quickly and help! It's a race against time to help everyone. They need to find out who is sick, help them get better, and stop the sickness from spreading to more people. It's a very big job, like solving a giant puzzle where every piece is important.

A Big "Health Emergency"

When many people get sick from the same germ, and it spreads fast, the grown-ups call it a "health emergency." This means it's a super important situation where everyone needs to work together very fast to solve the problem. It's like when there's a fire, and the brave firefighters rush quickly to put it out and keep everyone safe. In a health emergency, doctors and health workers rush to help sick people and stop the tricky germ from spreading to others. They know what to do and they work very, very hard, sometimes all day and all night. **Did you know?** Countries all over the world have special health helpers and organizations who train very hard for many years to keep everyone safe and healthy, not just in their own country, but across the globe! They are like a big, helpful team.

How Does Ebola Spread?

This is a very important part to understand! Ebola does not spread easily through the air when someone coughs or sneezes, like a common cold or the flu. That's a very good thing and makes it easier to stop! You mostly get it if you touch a sick person's blood or other body fluids (like sweat, throw-up, or spit), or if you touch things they have just touched that have these fluids on them. That's why grown-ups always tell us not to touch our faces or put our hands in our mouths after touching things, especially if our hands are not clean. That's also why doctors and nurses who help people with Ebola wear very special suits, gloves, and masks. They look like colorful or white superheroes in their protective gear! This helps them to not accidentally touch anything that might have the germ on it, so they stay safe while helping others.

Stopping the Germ: Super Powers!

The best way to fight this tricky germ, and many other germs that make us sick, is with super powers! And guess what? You already have some of these super powers yourself! It's not magic, it's science! One of the very best super powers you have is washing your hands! When you wash your hands with soap and water, you are like a superhero washing away all the tiny, invisible germs. The soap helps to trap the germs, and the water washes them down the drain. It's a simple act, but it's very powerful and keeps you healthy! **Did you know?** You should wash your hands for at least 20 seconds! That's about how long it takes to sing the 'Happy Birthday' song two times through, or to sing your favorite short song! Try it next time you wash your hands, and make sure to scrub all over, even between your fingers!

Who are the Heroes Helping?

There are so many brave doctors, kind nurses, and wonderful health workers in DR Congo right now. They are like real-life superheroes, working day and night to help the sick people get better and stronger. They give them medicine, clean water, and make sure they are comfortable. They also teach everyone in the community how to stay safe and stop the germ from spreading to their friends and family. They show people how to wash their hands properly and how to take care of each other in a safe way. Many kind people and organizations from all over Africa and the rest of the world are sending help too. They send things like special medicines, clean water, and special equipment to protect the health workers. Everyone is trying to help our friends in DR Congo, showing that we are all one big family.

What Can Kids Do?

Even though this is happening far away for many of us, there are important things you can do to help and understand: * **Wash your hands!** Always remember to wash your hands often with soap and water, especially before you eat, after you play, after you sneeze or cough, and after you use the toilet. This keeps *you* healthy and helps stop *all* kinds of germs. * **Be kind!** Think about the people in DR Congo. We can send them happy thoughts and hope that they get better soon. Being kind to others, even if they are far away, makes the world a better place for everyone. * **Learn!** Don't be afraid to ask your parents or teachers questions if you want to know more about germs or how to stay healthy. Asking questions and learning about the world makes you smart and helps you understand things better. * **Don't be scared!** Remember that doctors and scientists are very smart people. They have learned so much about germs and how they work, and they know how to fight them. They are always working to find new ways to keep us safe and healthy. **Did you know?** There are special medicines and vaccines (which are like a shield shot that helps your body fight germs) that help protect people from getting very sick from germs like Ebola! Scientists are working hard to make sure everyone can get them, so we can all be protected.

A Message of Hope

It's okay if hearing about big sicknesses makes you feel a little worried, but always remember this: many smart and kind people are working together, as a team, to help our friends in DR Congo. They are helping the sick people get well, and they are working very hard to stop the germ from spreading to more people. With everyone's help, DR Congo will be strong again, and the tricky Ebola germ will be stopped! Just like when we work together, we can solve big problems. Let's all keep washing our hands, being kind, and staying super healthy! We are all part of one big African family, and we look out for each other. Thank you for listening to the news today!
summary: There is a health emergency in DR Congo because many people are getting sick from a tricky germ called Ebola, but brave doctors and helpers are working very hard to stop it and keep everyone safe and healthy.
whyMatters: This is important because when people get sick, especially from a serious germ like Ebola, we all need to understand what is happening and how we can help keep ourselves and others healthy, like washing our hands and supporting the brave doctors and nurses who are working as heroes.

originalTitle: WHO declares Ebola outbreak in DR Congo an international emergency
title: WHO declares Ebola outbreak in DR Congo an international emergency
content: The agency added that the outbreak, with around 246 cases and 80 deaths, does not meet the criteria of pandemic emergency.
